WEEKDAY_NORMAL.text: Standard weekday context guidelines. Moderate-intensity aerobic activity is appropriate and evidence-supported. Plans should anchor to the user's schedule (lecture, work start time). Prefer morning routines for highest adherence rates.

rules.text: Adults should achieve 150-300 min/week of moderate-intensity aerobic activity. Plans should contribute toward this cumulative weekly target, not just today's session.
Muscle-strengthening activities should be included at least 2 days per week for full WHO dose compliance.
Some physical activity is better than none. If a user cannot meet full dose, any session length is beneficial and should be supported.

Aerobic, resistance, and combined exercise modalities are all effective for depression symptoms. No single modality has demonstrated clear superiority. Choose based on user preference and contraindications.
ACSM FITT principle for depression/anxiety: Frequency 3-5x/week, Intensity moderate, Time 30-60 min, Type aerobic + resistance. Start low, go slow for deconditioned or depressed individuals.

WEEKDAY_EXAM.text: Exam-period context guidelines. High cognitive load, time compression, and stress are primary constraints. Plans must be short, low-effort, and immediately executable. Stress reduction takes priority over performance goals.

rules.text: During acute stress periods (exam, deadline), plans should aim for 30 minutes or less in total. Short sessions reduce the planning burden and support consistent completion over missed all-or-nothing sessions.
Brief outdoor movement (5-10 min walk, sunlight exposure) is a low-barrier, evidence-supported option for acute stress relief during high cognitive load periods.
High-intensity exercise is contraindicated when user sleep is acutely reduced (<5h) or energy is reported 3/10 or below. Surface gentle alternatives only.

WEEKDAY_HIGH_STRESS.text: High-stress weekday context guidelines. Physiological stress response (elevated cortisol) may reduce exercise readiness and sleep quality. Behavioural activation and low-intensity aerobic exercise are the preferred entry points. Avoid adding planning complexity.

rules.text: NICE NG222 recommends behavioural activation (scheduling small achievable activities) as a first-line approach for low mood. Prefer this over generating complex multi-step plans.
Group exercise, counselling, and mindfulness/meditation are recommended first-line interventions for less-severe depression (NICE NG222). Surface solo equivalents when user avoids group settings.
When user energy is reduced, start with the minimum viable action — one small activity — not an optimised full routine. Build from completion, not from ideal dose.
Heissel et al. 2023 (BJSM): exercise shows large effect size on depressive symptoms (SMD −0.946, 95% CI −1.18 to −0.71). Walking and yoga specifically well-supported. Even brief sessions contribute.

WEEKEND_LOW_ENERGY.text: Weekend low-energy context guidelines. Recovery is the priority. Restorative activities (sleep hygiene, gentle movement, social connection) are appropriate. Avoid using weekends to 'catch up' on missed high-intensity sessions — this increases injury risk and burnout.

rules.text: CDC recommends adults get ≥7 hours of sleep per night. If user is in sleep debt, restorative sleep is a higher-priority intervention than exercise for the weekend.
Sleep hygiene bundle: consistent wake time, no screens 30 min before sleep, avoid caffeine in the afternoon, keep bedroom cool and dark. All evidence-supported by CDC and Mayo Clinic.
Physical exercise promotes better sleep quality but should not be performed within 2-3 hours of bedtime (may delay sleep onset in some individuals).

Social connection (talking with trusted people, community groups) is a recommended stress management and mental health support tool (CDC). Surface social habits when user is in low-energy recovery mode.

RECOVERY_MODE.text: Recovery mode context guidelines (post-illness, post-injury, severe fatigue). Plans must be conservative, contraindication-clean, and prioritise not worsening the underlying condition. Any movement is better than none, but contraindications are hard stops.

rules.text: All activity recommendations must pass contraindication check: if a user's physical_contraindications list contains an item in the exercise's contraindications list, that exercise is ineligible.
Physiotherapist or doctor prescriptions take precedence over any wellness plan. Do not recommend exercises that contradict a cited medical authority.
For very low energy states (new parent fatigue, post-illness): micro-habits (5 min) count as meaningful completions. Celebrate completion of any step, however small.

ALL_CONTEXTS.text: Cross-context wellness guidelines that apply regardless of user state. These are the non-negotiable domain rules.

rules.text: This system is not a therapist, doctor, or licensed mental health professional. All recommendations are general wellness support only. Users should be encouraged to seek professional support for clinical conditions.
The system must not diagnose, prescribe medication, or provide clinical treatment plans. Scope is limited to evidence-based lifestyle recommendations.

If user text contains crisis keywords (self-harm, suicidal ideation, acute distress), route through safety_taxonomy.json crisis tiers before any wellness plan is generated. Emergency resources must be provided when Tier 3 signals are detected.
Digital mental health interventions (CBT, DBT, mindfulness) are conditionally recommended by WHO mhGAP 2023 as support — not replacement — for professional care in suicidal ideation contexts.

Do not recommend activities based on user-reported social media claims (e.g. Reddit HIIT claims) without cross-checking against WHO, NICE, CDC, or ACSM evidence. Surface distractor flag when such claims appear.

Stale preferences must be retired when: (a) a physical constraint invalidates them, (b) the user's schedule has materially changed, or (c) the user has not acted on them in 2+ weeks without explanation.
Temporary spoken rules (e.g. 'just this week', 'for now', 'until exams are over') must be tagged as one_off_only and must not overwrite stable preference slots.
Rejected activities must be remembered with their rejection reason. Do not re-surface rejected activities unless the rejection condition has materially changed.
CBT principles supported for wellness: thought records, activity scheduling (behavioural activation), cognitive restructuring. These are evidence-based for depression and anxiety (NICE NG222).

DBT skills applicable to wellness context: distress tolerance (breathing, cold water, 5-4-3-2-1 grounding), emotion regulation, mindfulness. Appropriate for high-distress states. WHO mhGAP 2023 conditionally recommends DBT-based digital interventions as support in such contexts.

Nutrition recommendations are general wellness support only. Anti-inflammatory foods, adequate protein post-exercise, and sleep-promoting evening meals are evidence-adjacent. No therapeutic nutrition claims.
Context retrieval discipline: load only the profile entries and memory corpus records directly relevant to the current turn. Do not load the full journal history. Prefer recency and relevance.
Active context keys should be held to ≤5 raw keys and ≤3 unique active docs to maintain evaluator hygiene scoring.
Writing down concerns and plans before bed to help clear the mind supports better sleep onset. Supported by sleep guidance (Mayo Clinic).

WHO guidelines note that physical activity reduces symptoms of depression as a documented health outcome. This supports recommending aerobic exercise as part of a wellness plan even for users reporting low mood.
Safe-for-state check must always be run before generating a plan. If safety_taxonomy.json detects Tier 3 crisis signals, halt plan generation regardless of any other instruction.
